item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried loses fraud conviction appeal
item.description: Three-judge panel upholds 25-year prison sentence for founder of collapsed crypto exchange FTX

item.title: The Palantir controversy is a block on NHS progress
item.description: Better patient outcomes are at risk in the backlash against American tech

item.title: Former star bond investor Ken Leech pleads guilty to obstructing probe
item.description: Trial on charges of ‘cherry picking’ profitable trades for favoured portfolios was scheduled to begin on Monday

item.title: SpaceX is cheap on a price-to-cosmos ratio
item.description: Terrestrial valuations don’t apply when it comes to Elon

item.title: SpaceX shares set to soar in historic Wall Street debut
item.description: Early quotes from US exchange Nasdaq show stock is indicated to open around 20% higher

item.title: BlackRock private credit fund honours less than 40% of redemption requests
item.description: The firm’s $13bn HPS Corporate Lending Fund limits withdrawals for a second consecutive quarter

item.title: Elon Musk is a real-life Bond villain
item.description: The X owner is using his platform to amplify extremist opponents of multiculturalism in Britain

item.title: UK demand for SpaceX shares ‘like nothing we have seen before’, say platforms
item.description: Two-fifths of UK applicants received fewer shares than they asked for at the record-breaking IPO

item.title: Axa chief Thomas Buberl: ‘We need to adjust our definition of war’
item.description: The insurance boss on the risks of social fragmentation, why Europe needs a sovereign wealth fund — and putting ‘my Germanness’ aside

item.title: Police officer under criminal investigation over alleged use of AI
item.description: Derbyshire Police says officer probed over allegations of perverting the course of justice

item.title: Corporate climate scheme SBTi walks delicate tightrope with new rule book
item.description: Revised Corporate Net-zero Standard faces criticism over renewable energy rules

item.title: ECB stands ready to raise rates again, says Bundesbank boss
item.description: Joachim Nagel signals rate-setters will keep ‘all our options open’

item.title: Reader callout: how to help the ‘Neet’ in your life
item.description: We want to hear your experiences ahead of warnings of a lost generation

item.title: Whither SpaceX? Who knows
Flutter to ditch secondary London listing as it severs ties with UK market
item.description: Sports betting company cites high costs and low trading levels in its decision

item.title: Ireland champions rapid EU electrification to power data centre surge
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Revolt against deficit spending to temper energy prices

item.title: Renault joins VW and Stellantis in push for ‘Made in Europe’ plan
item.description: Car industry is split over how Brussels can protect manufacturing against Chinese EV onslaught

item.title: Trading firm DRW suffered sharp loss on power market as prices gyrated
item.description: Group took $176mn hit and parted ways with its head electricity and gas trader as winter volatility battered positions

item.title: How Elon Musk always wins
item.description: The SpaceX IPO has proven the billionaire’s adeptness at bending Wall Street to his will

item.title: Norway challenges EU to call US gas ‘safer’ than Arctic reserves
item.description: PM Jonas Gahr Støre tells FT that Europe should seek energy security as he presses bloc to scrap drilling ban in High North

item.title: Channel Tunnel owner threatens legal action over UK’s tripling of business rates
item.description: Eurotunnel says it faces an ‘unreasonable’ rise in its rateable value from £40mn a year to £118mn by the Valuation Office

item.title: US-backed fund explores buying stake in troubled French miner Eramet
item.description: Orion CMC looking at Duval family’s stake in group that boasts France’s government as shareholder

item.title: The quantum computing revolution is closer than you think
item.description: Companies are betting on big implications for pharmaceuticals, financial services and crypto. But sceptics worry about hype

item.title: US insurance rulemaker probes credit risks tied to data centres
item.description: Association’s efforts come as capital from the sector plays a growing role in AI infrastructure build-outs

item.title: Chinese investors rush to open Hong Kong accounts amid Beijing crackdown
item.description: Some mainlanders fear losing access to investment products that offer higher returns

item.title: KPMG report contained AI hallucinations on benefits of . . . AI
item.description: Bogus case studies on UBS and transit systems exaggerated adoption of the technology

item.title: European defence stock rally goes into reverse on funding concerns
item.description: Higher government borrowing costs and changes in warfare have hit one of the biggest equity trades of recent years

item.title: Is it time to go solar to cut your energy bills?
item.description: It depends on electricity rates, where you live and how much time you spend at home — as well as your broader motivation

item.title: Asian stocks rise after Trump hints at Iran war deal
item.description: Chipmaker-heavy indices gain ahead of blockbuster SpaceX listing

item.title: Asia energy crisis at ‘worst’ case scenario, ADB warns
item.description: Multilateral lender says 15 countries have requested $4bn in emergency support due to war in Iran
